threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when a bus driver shouts 'buses only!' at pedestrians. It’s either that or you think it's a warning.
Last night, a bloke on the 36 bus swore he spotted a badger roaming near the Bussey Building. Only in Peckham, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a street musician playing a theremin outside the supermarket while a toddler danced to the sound of sirens. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a full bar inside an abandoned subway station, complete with a DJ spinning under the flickering lights of old transit signage.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by how they navigate the alleys—swiftly dodging those wild street art battles while still holding a burrito.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni driver serenading passengers with an old jazz tune. For a moment, it felt like we were all in the same rhythm, city’s heartbeat.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that many Gràcia cafes close for a few hours in the afternoon. They think it's just siesta vibes, but it’s a chance to reset before the evening bustle.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the time it takes for a metro train to arrive on L2, even during rush hour.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'Columbia Road Flower Market' instead of just 'the market'—it's like you're asking for a direction back to the suburbs.
Heard a group on the N37 arguing over who lost a shoe. Turned out it was all for a bet on some match. Night buses have the best stories.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a mismatched café opening at dawn, with a dog casually perched on the counter, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, I saw a café where the barista served coffee in vintage porcelain from thrift shops. Each cup tells a story—such a quirky touch!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their ability to navigate the dodgy construction zones while still dodging the taco trucks at lunchtime.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a woman give her seat to an elderly man on the 14 Mission while the driver serenaded us with old R&B hits. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just stroll into any bar and find tapas, but locals know to avoid the tourist traps around Plaça del Sol. Hidden gems are often just streets away.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still don’t know about the €1.50 menu del día at the small, tucked-away spots; they flock to the tourist traps instead.
